response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings 15+ years of broad software development experience, open-source contributions, and Linux familiarity that partially align with the role's technical expectations. However, two critical disqualifiers make him a poor fit: the job requires German at minimum C1 proficiency, while the candidate self-reports only 'Basic' German — a hard requirement for an IT consulting firm almost certainly operating in a German-speaking market. Additionally, the role is an executive-level leadership position requiring proven team management, IT architecture responsibility, UML expertise, and client advisory experience, none of which appear substantively in the resume; his career has remained largely in individual-contributor or senior-developer tracks.
strengths: Broad, multi-language technical depth across 15+ years spanning web, mobile, embedded, and data-intensive systems
Demonstrated open-source commitment (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial) with Linux and distributed-systems exposure
Experience in regulated/compliance-adjacent domains (fintech at RIDE Capital, high-volume data pipelines at Dunnhumby)
concerns: German proficiency is self-rated 'Basic', far below the mandatory C1 minimum — a non-negotiable requirement for client-facing consulting work
No demonstrated team leadership, line management, or project ownership at organizational scale; all roles are individual-contributor or technical contributor
No mention of UML, formal IT architecture frameworks, requirements management, or risk/compliance advisory — core competencies listed in the job description
